title: PlausiDen — the platform
description: Six tools, one typed substrate. Forge, Loom, Crawler, Annotator, CMS, LFI.

sections.title: Six tools. One substrate.
sections.lede: Every PlausiDen tool shares the same typed contracts. Compose what you need; ignore what you don't.

sections.text: The typed build pipeline. 27 audit phases run on every build: tokens, CSP, a11y landmarks, semantic HTML, theme contrast, phantom-button detection, unbuilt-route detection, external-asset blocking, jurisdiction compliance, label consistency, link check, locale html-lang, motion preferences, path consistency, perf budget, print stylesheet, reader safety, render correctness, required pages, self-check, SEO, SRI, structured data, theme consistency, viewport audit. Every commit produces a Merkle-chained Ed25519-signed report.

The typed UI primitive library. Closed-enum CmsSection variants render through a single render_page() function. 13 themes including AMOLED-true dark and WCAG-AA-targeted high-contrast pair. OKLCH-based palette, system-font stack, dual-theme by default, reduced-motion honoured automatically.

The runtime auditor. Walks the rendered DOM with a headless browser, runs 120+ typed detectors against the actual page state, emits a journey schema that Forge consumes as a phase. Catches what static analysis cannot — focus traps in modals, hydration mismatches, broken aria-controls links, layout shifts under real network conditions.

Operator capture. Records what your users actually do — typed event stream, lossless replay, local-first storage, opt-in by user. No cloud upload, no third-party telemetry. Use it to derive new audit detectors, debug a regression, or hand a session to support.

Content as typed data. Your pages are JSON files in version control. Sections are closed-enum variants validated at parse time. No database, no editor lock-in, no migration scripts when your schema evolves — types are the migration.

AI evaluation, not generation. The Critic trait gates every AI proposal — Claude, Gemini, GPT, or a locally-run model — through a typed Decision: Accept, Reject, or Refine with explicit regeneration guidance. Pluggable provider, pluggable critic. Today: LLM-only Critic ships immediately. Tomorrow: neurosymbolic LFI Critic drops in via a feature flag, zero changes at the call site.

items.hint: Gated at build time, not retrofitted.
Functional suitability, performance, compatibility, usability, reliability, security, maintainability, portability.

sections.title: Use one. Or all six.
sections.lede: PlausiDen is opinionated about types and unopinionated about adoption. Bring just Forge for typed CMS builds, or layer Loom + Crawler + LFI for full coverage.
